level_2.origins_history:
Garnacha Tinta (Grenache Noir in France) is one of the world's most widely planted red grape varieties, originating in the Aragon region of northeastern Spain. DNA analysis confirms Spanish origin, contradicting earlier theories of Sardinian roots.

The variety spread throughout the western Mediterranean during the expansion of the Kingdom of Aragon. It became the dominant variety in the southern Rhône Valley and remains central to wines from Châteauneuf-du-Pape, Côtes du Rhône, and Priorat.

Garnacha produces wines ranging from pale, ethereal expressions to powerful, concentrated examples depending on yields and winemaking. The variety excels in hot, dry climates where its late ripening and drought resistance are advantages.

genetic_lineage.overview: Garnacha Tinta originated in Aragon, Spain and has given rise to numerous color mutations.

parents.name: Unknown
parents.notes: No parentage established. Indigenous to Aragon, Spain.

mutations.description: White-skinned mutation widely planted in southern France.

genetic_lineage.genetic_significance: Garnacha's numerous color mutations and widespread cultivation make it central to Mediterranean viticulture.

clones.name: Clone 70
clones.characteristics: Standard French clone with good color and structure.

clones.name: Clone 362
clones.characteristics: Lower yielding clone with more concentrated fruit.

clones.name: Priorat Old Vine Selection
clones.characteristics: Massal selections from century-old vines. Intensely concentrated with low yields.

terroir.effect: The defining soil of Priorat. Dark slate retains heat and produces deeply concentrated, mineral wines.
Iconic rounded stones of Châteauneuf-du-Pape. Retains daytime heat, reflecting warmth onto grapes.
Found in Campo de Borja. Produces more aromatic, earlier-drinking wines.

susceptibilities.notes: Tight clusters can trap moisture in humid conditions.
susceptibilities.impact_on_wine: Rarely problematic in typical dry growing regions

susceptibilities.notes: Can suffer poor fruit set in cool, wet flowering conditions.

resistances.notes: Exceptional drought tolerance from deep root systems.
Tolerates the Mistral and Tramontana winds.

disease_profile.general_notes: Garnacha is a late-ripening variety that produces pale-colored wines with high alcohol potential. The variety oxidizes easily, requiring careful handling in the winery. Old vines with restricted yields produce the most concentrated wines.

regional_expressions.style_summary: Spain's most prestigious Garnacha region, producing powerful, mineral wines from old vines on steep slate slopes. The combination of extreme terroir and low yields creates wines of remarkable concentration and longevity.

typical_character.aromatics: Blackberry, slate mineral, herbs, licorice, balsamic

regional_expressions.style_summary: The southern Rhône's most famous appellation, where Grenache dominates blends with Syrah and Mourvèdre. Wines combine power with aromatic complexity, showing characteristic garrigue herbs and spice.

typical_character.aromatics: Raspberry, garrigue herbs, lavender, spice, leather
